items.title: Man City agree deal to sign Anderson
items.summary: Manchester City have agreed a deal with Nottingham Forest to sign Elliot Anderson.

items.title: Man City agree record fee with Forest for Anderson
items.summary: Manchester City agree a club record deal with Nottingham Forest to sign England midfielder Elliot Anderson.

items.title: Anderson's journey to England star and potential record signing
items.summary: Elliot Anderson has emerged as a key part of Thomas Tuchel's England side and is now on the brink of a big money move to Manchester City.

items.title: Pepe double vs Curacao sends Ivory Coast through
items.summary: Former Arsenal forward Nicolas Pepe scored twice as Ivory Coast secured second-place in Group E with a comfortable 2-0 win over Curacao.

items.title: Injury-hit Warrington edge Catalans to move level on points with Leeds
items.summary: ​​​​​​Injury-hit Warrington Wolves moved level on points with Super League table toppers Leeds Rhinos after eking out a narrow 18-16 victory over Catalans Dragons.

items.title: Man Utd's Onana set for Trabzonspor loan
items.summary: Manchester United goalkeeper Andre Onana is close to securing a second loan spell at Turkish club Trabzonspor.

items.title: Ecuador stun Germany to reach knockouts
items.summary: Ecuador stunned already-qualified Germany with a 2-1 comeback win in New Jersey to reach the World Cup knockout stages and dent Scotland’s round of 32 hopes.

items.title: Ecuador fight back to upset Germany
items.summary: Goals from Nilson Angulo and Gonzalo Plata help Ecuador come from behind to beat Germany 2-1 to qualify for the knockout stages of the World Cup, as one of the top eight third-placed teams.

items.title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
items.summary: With the final round of group fixtures under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

items.title: Ivory Coast defeat Curacao to progress to knockout stages for first time
items.summary: Ivory Coast ease past Curacao in their World Cup Group E clash courtesy of a Nicolas Pepe brace, confirming their qualification in the knockout stages of the competition for the first time in their history.

items.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
items.summary: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

items.title: Plata’s golden touch against Germany sends Ecuador into World Cup last 32
items.summary: Sí, se puede – yes, we can – was the chant that rang out from the 55,000 Ecuador fans as their team’s final group match kicked off. items.title: Côte d’Ivoire into World Cup knockouts for first time as Pépé finishes off Curaçao
items.summary: Côte d’Ivoire surviving the World Cup group stage for the first time, in the year of our football gods 2026, is one of those titbits that sounds like it shouldn’t be true, and yet here we are. items.summary: NEYMAR has reportedly pulled out of a potential move to FC Cincinnati after becoming irritated during negotiations. items.summary: ECUADOR boss Sebastian Beccacece delivered a World Cup celebration for the ages after shocking Germany.
